characters.physical_details: Six-one, stocky, ruddy-faced. Receding hairline, big hands, gap between front teeth. Expensive casual clothes that look cheap on purpose.

key_relationships.paul_decker: Best friend since freshman year at University of Minnesota. Martin never told Paul about the fire.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Bound by the 2002 fire and Ray Toomey's death. Twenty-two years of protection, obligation, and genuine complicated love. Left Tommy the lake house in his will.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Fraternity brothers. Martin sleeping with Joel's ex-wife Karen for eight months. Joel found out one month before the draft.
key_relationships.cliff_haywood: Owes Cliff forty thousand from a failed joint real estate deal two years ago.
key_relationships.nate_griggs: Uncomplicated friendship. Tolerates the podcast persona.
key_relationships.doug_whitfield: One of the few who noticed Doug's weight loss and asked about the divorce.

characters.occupation_status: Financial consultant, self-employed. Inherited the lake house from his parents. Divorced from Linda five years ago. No kids.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Wrote the handwritten fire account in clear, detailed prose.

state_transitions.change: Dies of cardiac arrest — undiagnosed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy exacerbated by alcohol, sildenafil, and hot tub heat stress. Found dead in bed.

characters.availability: Alive in Chapter 1 only. Present in memory throughout.

characters.physical_details: Five-ten, thin, wire-rimmed glasses. Careful grooming. Clean-shaven.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Best friend twenty-five years. Martin was Paul's best man. The will revealing Tommy as heir breaks Paul's understanding of the friendship.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Friendly but not close. Paul becomes suspicious of Martin-Tommy bond during investigation.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Growing alliance. Investigative partners in the final act.

characters.occupation_status: Insurance claims adjuster, Minneapolis. Married to Anne. Two teenagers.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Insurance and risk assessment terminology.

state_transitions.change: Tampers — repositions Martin's body, wipes surfaces touched during CPR.
Discovers Martin left the lake house to Tommy. Grief shifts to suspicion.
Confronts Tommy. Decides not to protect him the way Martin did.

characters.physical_details: Five-nine, soft in the middle, expressive face, talks with hands. Wears a podcast-branded cap the others mock.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Genuine friendship. Nate's voicemail mocking Martin's draft picks — using death language — becomes his nightmare.

characters.occupation_status: Fantasy sports podcaster ('Griggs on the Grid'). Small audience, unreliable revenue. Girlfriend Mia not present.

state_transitions.change: Discovers Martin's body. Had left a group voicemail six hours earlier mocking draft picks ('you're dead to me').
Tampers — deletes voicemail from Martin's phone.
Accidentally references the deleted voicemail during Mallick's interview.

characters.physical_details: Six-two, still athletic, crew cut graying at temples. Big hands always gripping something. Scar on left forearm from the 2002 fire.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Bound by the fire. Martin's protection kept Tommy coming to the draft for twenty-two years. Martin left him the lake house.
key_relationships.cliff_haywood: Cliff discovers the fire document and leverages Tommy. Tommy kills Cliff.

characters.occupation_status: High school football coach and PE teacher, suburban Minneapolis. Married to Denise. Three kids (two high school, one college).

state_transitions.change: Tampers — removes and flushes Viagra pills from Martin's nightstand.
Cliff leverages the fire document. Tommy agrees but shifts internally toward violence.
Kills Cliff at the dock — strikes head, holds under water. Stages as drowning but gets the shoes wrong.
Confesses to Cliff's murder. Arrested.

characters.availability: Present throughout. POV chapters 3, 12. Not POV after Ch 14.

characters.physical_details: Five-eleven, dressed better than everyone — pressed chinos, collared polos. Permanent tan. Silver hair. Firm handshake.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Martin owed him forty thousand. Friendship strained by debt.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Discovers fire document, leverages Tommy. Does not understand the leverage is lethal.

characters.occupation_status: Real estate developer. Business failing — three projects stalled, one in default. Divorced twice. The forty thousand is survival money.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Real estate contracts and financial documents.

state_transitions.change: Tampers — enters Martin's office, handles financial documents. Discovers safe is accessible.
Opens safe, finds fire document about Tommy and Ray Toomey. Photographs it.
Confronts Tommy with leverage.
Killed by Tommy at the dock. Found by Doug floating face-down.

characters.physical_details: Six feet, gaunt from recent weight loss. Bags under eyes. Looks older than forty-seven. Hoodies and sweatpants.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Martin was one of few who noticed Doug's decline.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Share a genuine quiet moment on the dock/deck — the most human exchange in the book's middle section.

characters.occupation_status: Pharmaceutical sales rep. Recently divorced from Steph (three months ago). On escitalopram (Lexapro) and lorazepam (Ativan). No children.

state_transitions.change: Tampers — removes prescription bottles from shared upstairs bathroom.
Interior life revealed: depression, medication, divorce shame.
Discovers Cliff's body at the dock. His scream ends the comedy.

characters.physical_details: Five-ten, lean, wiry. Looks younger than forty-seven. Sharp features, quick eyes.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Fraternity brothers. Joel found out Martin was sleeping with ex-wife Karen one month before the draft. Came to confront Martin. Martin died first.
key_relationships.karen_beckman: Ex-wife, divorced two years. Now with Martin. Joel's anger is at Martin, not Karen.
key_relationships.paul_decker: Investigative partners in the final act.

characters.occupation_status: Public defender, Hennepin County. Daughter Ellie, sophomore at University of Wisconsin.

state_transitions.change: Becomes prime suspect — last to argue with Martin, refuses to explain, didn't tamper (looks calculated).
Reveals Martin was sleeping with Karen. Suspicion dissipates.
Joel and Paul discover fire document and identify Tommy as killer.

characters.availability: Present throughout. POV chapters 4, 9, 16. The sixth man — didn't tamper.

characters.physical_details: Five-six, stocky. Gray-streaked brown hair in a low bun. Reading glasses on a beaded chain. Blazer over a polo. Carries a leather notebook and a casserole dish.

key_relationships.all_guests: Professional subjects. Treats each correction as a data point.

characters.occupation_status: County detective, Becker County Sheriff's Office, Detroit Lakes, MN. Twenty years on the force. Lives alone with two cats.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Forensic report literacy. Interview technique.

state_transitions.change: Arrives with casserole. Establishes folksy persona.
Realizes multiple people tampered independently.
Returns for Cliff's murder. Drops the folksy persona entirely.
Receives Tommy's confession. Arrests him.

characters.availability: Arrives Ch 4. Departs and returns Ch 15. POV chapters 5, 10, 15, 18.

timeline.story_start: Thursday, mid-September 2024. Arrival day for the twenty-fifth annual fantasy football draft weekend.
timeline.estimated_span: Seven days — Thursday through the following Wednesday.

seasonal_track.season: Early-to-mid September in northern Minnesota. Warm afternoons (60s-70s F), cool mornings (40s-50s F). Leaves beginning to turn. Golden afternoon light on the lake.

key_events.event: Deputies arrive; Mallick arrives with casserole
Cliff finds fire document in safe
Paul finds will — Tommy inherits house
Tommy kills Cliff at the dock

geography.primary_setting: Martin Kessler's lake house on Elbow Lake, rural Becker County, Minnesota.

key_locations.name: Main floor
key_locations.details: A-frame. Great room with vaulted ceiling, stone fireplace, open kitchen (table seats eight). Martin's study off the main hallway. Half bath near front door. Front faces gravel drive; back opens onto wraparound deck. Draft board hangs on great room wall.

key_locations.name: Upper floor
key_locations.details: Master bedroom (Martin's), two guest bedrooms sharing a full bathroom. Third step from top of stairs creaks audibly — everyone knows to skip it.

key_locations.name: Basement
key_locations.details: Finished: TV, card table, two pull-out sofas, full bath, laundry. Nate and Doug sleep here.

key_locations.name: Martin's study
key_locations.details: Ground floor. Desk, filing cabinet, bookshelves. Wall safe behind a lake landscape print — combination dial, Martin left it one tick off from locked. Contains: financial records, will, cash, manila envelope labeled 'RT.'

key_locations.name: Deck and hot tub
key_locations.details: Wraparound wooden deck on the lake side. Hot tub seats six. Adirondack chairs. Propane grill. Where most group conversations happen.

key_locations.name: The dock
key_locations.details: Wooden, thirty feet into Elbow Lake. Swim platform at end. Small bench. Water beside the dock is roughly three feet deep. Where Cliff is found dead.

key_locations.name: Firepit area
key_locations.details: Stone-ringed pit between house and dock. Adirondack chairs. Evening conversations.

distances: Lake house to Dent, MN (nearest town): ~15 minutes by car
Lake house to Detroit Lakes (county seat, sheriff's office): ~30 minutes
Deck to dock: 80-yard stone path through pine and birch
Great room to study: down the hallway, two doors past the half bath

world_rules: Becker County Sheriff's jurisdiction. Small department. Serious forensics must be requested from the BCA (Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension).
Cell service: spotty at the house — better on the deck and near the road, worse inside and at the dock.
Nearest hospital: Detroit Lakes, thirty minutes. Ambulance response ~25-30 minutes.
Minnesota September: variable weather, can shift from warm to cold quickly.
Fantasy football league: keeper league (retain up to three players yearly). Martin's team auto-drafted when he died.

power_structure.mechanism: The fire document — handwritten account of the 2002 fire that killed Ray Toomey. Not blackmail but a bond of obligation.
The fire document discovered, photographed, and used as leverage.
Legal authority. Folksy persona masks it until Ch 15.
Only person who understands criminal procedure. Operational power in final act.
Will leaving lake house to Tommy reshapes Paul's understanding and catalyzes Tommy's breakdown.

objects.name: The fire document
objects.origin: Handwritten August 2002. Kept in lake house safe twenty-two years.
objects.status: Discovered by Cliff (Ch 8), photographed, used as leverage (Ch 12), found by Joel/Paul (Ch 16), found by Mallick (Ch 15-18).

objects.name: Wall safe
objects.status: Combination lock, habitually left one tick off from locked. Contains: records, will, cash, envelope labeled 'RT.'

objects.name: Martin's phone
objects.status: Voicemail deleted by Nate. In evidence by Ch 4.

objects.name: Viagra pills
objects.origin: Joke gift. One pill given Friday evening.
objects.status: Remaining pills flushed by Tommy (Ch 3). One dropped in hallway and retrieved.

objects.name: Doug's prescription bottles
objects.status: Removed from shared bathroom, placed in duffel bag in basement.

objects.name: Nate's recording device
objects.status: Used draft night and intermittently. Ambient audio becomes evidence Ch 15-18.

objects.name: Cliff's phone
objects.status: Contains photos of fire document and texts to lawyer about 'leverage.' Found by Mallick after death.

objects.name: Martin's will
objects.status: Found by Paul Ch 11. Tommy inherits the lake house.

objects.name: The casserole
objects.status: Brought Ch 4, refrigerated, untouched at novel's end. Symbol of the investigation's two modes.

objects.name: Draft board
objects.origin: Physical whiteboard on great room wall.
objects.status: Martin's picks circled in red. Final image of the novel.

objects.name: Cliff's shoes (evidence)
objects.origin: Leather loafers — his house shoes, not dock shoes.
objects.status: Found on body. Wrong shoes signal staging. Key forensic detail.

debts.deadline: No formal deadline — informal debt from failed joint real estate deal, two years ago
debts.status: Owed by Martin's estate. Unresolved at novel's end.

income_sources.reliability: The forty thousand is survival money, not portfolio money.

knowledge_state.knows: Tommy started the 2002 fire that killed Ray Toomey; Martin helped cover it up
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone except Tommy until document discovered

knowledge_state.knows: He started the fire; Martin has a written account in his safe
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until Cliff finds the document

knowledge_state.knows: Martin was sleeping with ex-wife Karen
Fire document — Tommy's role, Ray Toomey, Martin's cover-up
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone except Tommy (Ch 12) until found by others

knowledge_state.knows: Will leaves house to Tommy; suspects Martin was protecting Tommy
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Group until Paul shares with Joel Ch 16

knowledge_state.knows: Full picture — fire, document, leverage, Cliff's murder motive
Multiple tamperers (Ch 10); Cliff murdered (Ch 15); fire document (Ch 15-18)
He deleted voicemail; Mallick knows after Ch 6
His depression and medication; removed bottles from bathroom

environmental_constants: Elbow Lake: lapping water audible from deck and basement. Loons at dusk and dawn.
Pine-paneled walls smell of decades of wood smoke and lake damp. Third stair step creaks audibly.
Hot tub pump hums when running — audible from master bedroom and deck.
Kitchen: old refrigerator cycles loudly on a ~40-minute rhythm. Coffee maker gurgles. Where people go when they can't sleep.
September light: golden, low-angle afternoon sun on the lake. Trees beginning to turn.
Night sounds: loons, wind through pines, motorboat on far side of lake, refrigerator, house settling.
Martin's scotch (Lagavulin) smell persists in the great room after his death.
Gravel driveway — arriving vehicles heard before seen.
